item.title: Legendary Season’s Greetings!!
item.dc:creator: luvdechub

item.title: Spectacular and scary nature (and cat tax)
item.description:
A big nature dump for all the people needing a small distraction this holiday.
The Bat-Submersible
I want the cute armoured ball.
That's a too silly name for a dangerous plant. Also: Geez, Australia. Who hurt you? Yeah, I know it was the English, but still. Maybe chill just a little bit on the suicide-inducing plants, maybe?
It's not easy being a tree hugger in South America.
It did not choose the turd life, the turd life chose it.
TFW you really wanted to be an artist, but "doorman" is the only career option.
The bird equivalent to outside of a British pub on a Saturday night.
Something about living in glass houses, idk....
Nice overbite, dude.
I still can't quite get over how these guys in the hyena family are more closely related to cats than dogs.
See?! Cats!
My cat Charlie: "See! Big cats are also scared of leaves, it must be dangerous!"
Me: (Don't think it, don't think it, don't think it) My brain: It fucken wimdy!
Big(ish) cat doing small cat thing.
There's a 3 bird pile-up on Pine. To avoid foraging delays, please reroute to Elm.
When I found out that these little guys were called urchins, it gave a whole new perspective on the lyrics to 'Paradise City' by Guns n' Roses.
The future star of Tim Burton's next movie.
The world can't be all bad as long as we have bats eating grapes.
Cat tax: Ms. Charlotta has been wolfing down so much tuna this holiday, she's become a bit hoarse. We hope we haven't bugged you, and we wish you're having a bearable holiday. Merry Christmas , Frohe Weihnachten, Hyvä Joulu, Feliz Navidad, and most of all: God Jul from Sweden.
item.dc:creator: Kittensandyarns

item.title: Transition Timeline (plus memes for the cool, weird kids)
item.description:
Another year down, and what a long, strange trip it’s been. The road hasn’t been easy, but damn if it ain’t rewarding. Thanks for all the funnies and distractions and I truly hope all of you have the most beautiful holiday season. <3 Now, please enjoy a year-end dump of silly, vaguely horny memes
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
The real ones know
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
And finally, a very merry crisis from Lydia as she chills in her favorite place (a pile of warm laundry that clearly needs cat hair before I can put it away)
item.dc:creator: vhskitten

item.title: repost
item.dc:creator: atadrisque

item.title: Take that, Education!
item.description:
Short story time. Thanks to a post from @Shawnthegirl, < https://imgur.com/gallery/e0vtnHV > I learned that the utterly useless shitbag Elongated Muskrat fired verbal shots against Wikipedia, calling it "wokepedia". The fine ladies and gentlemen of our imgur community dug deeper and found out that the criticized "woke DEI" expenses were in fact resources allocated to spreading knowledge to other channels/media types besides plain text, and also for fighting the spreading of disinformation. This prompted me to spontaneously pump a hundred bucks (euros) into the biggest and most proof-read knowledge base of humanity which I also happen to use almost daily. How about you guys on the other snowball team use your precious pinecones to peg the muskbag royally in the behind? I invite you to - if you by any way have the means - donate to wikipedia and therefore help spreading knowledge and fight disinformation. And annoy the shit out of right wing dumbtards at the same time. Much love for you from Schermany
It's in German, but I promise it says "thank you for your donation of 100 € to bla"

item.title: A random assortment of older memes, gifs, and videos to break up your selfie day (XL "I just got home from all my family responsibilities and have time to myself" edition)
item.description:
We're all just big little kids when we're excited.
I enjoyed the book Snow Falling on Cedars. This isn't that, or close, even, but still.
It's multifaceted disappointment and self-loathing.
Siblings today, and every other day.
Heroes
I tell you hwat.
I enjoy
Ziggy was underrated in his day, and underappreciated every day after. I did get a Far Side desk calendar from my mom for Christmas though, so I've got that going for me, which is nice.
How quickly can you get through all five stages of grief? Maybe just four.
This is why you respect your QA people.
Respect for a perfect prank.
This is art. You will hear a buzzer. When you hear the buzzer, stare at the art.
You should now feel mentally reinvigorated.
The house knew.
You're probably planning to make a Taco Bell joke in the comments. I challenge you to make a better one.
This is Butterscotch.
Holiday break with kids.
Love me some good demos.
Short-lived trend, circa 2021, I think.
High risk, low reward.
Merry Christmas, ya filthy animals.
item.dc:creator: Fejerro

item.title: Crimbo Dump 07
item.dc:creator: Crazychristo

item.title: The Christmas Cat
item.description:
This is my fiance, Kat. Every year she researches old age homes for elderly folk whose family stopped visiting. Then she asks them what would they like for Christmas, and delivers it to them!
This year we made it to three different homes, and put a lot of smiles on old folks' faces. :)
Some can't remember making a wish, but are very happy for the visitation.
Neglect often leades to isolation, and so many old people will not have anyone visit them for Christmas, or bring presents. This is where Kat comes in!
I'm incredibly proud of her. It's heartbreaking to see how many elderly peeps are forgotten, left behind after leading full lives. She's already looking forward to next year, reaching out to more old age homes. So many elderly people could use a spark of joy in their lives. Fingers crossed the Christmas Cat (Weihnachtskatz in German) will continue to grow each year! TLDR: My girlfriend started a charity out of her own pocket to bring Christmas presents to neglected residents in old age homes.
item.dc:creator: badmanjam

item.dc:creator: Fejerro

item.description:
This is in the UK So about a month ago I made a post about me going to prison for a crime 2 years ago (drug possession). I talked about how I improved my life, got a great job, got clean, got my own place. What mitigating circumstances I had an that the prosecution wanted 2 years and that I plead guilty. I got a lot of love and support from you guys so I wanted to give a update. Given I live so far away from where the court is my lawyer asked for a fixed date, normally you only get 24hrs notice. They agreed and set my date at the maximum they could. Which I'm taking as a good sign because they don't see me as a risk or emergency to get me sentenced. Also I had a meeting with the probation office, I think I made my case clear, how much I improved and how I regret and feel ashamed for what I did. She layed out the 3 things they can do, recommend prison, recommend suspended sentence or community order. She talked a lot about the order at the end, about what that would entail, plus afterwards she contacted me to answer some medical questions to recommend me to a service for autistic people who have or may commit a offence. The catch is, this service is only for people not in prison so she can't be recommending prison. Most likely I'm going to have to pay a big fine, go to this service, go to mandatory therapy, maybe regular drug tests and a lot of visits from people to monitor me. Not the best outcome but not the worst either.
Random cat tax for the heavy topic.
item.dc:creator: thetinymonarch

item.title: Damage in Santa Cruz
item.description:
Waves battering the harbor.
The end of the wharf fell into the sea (and eventually that skidsteer, three people went with all that and were rescued unhurt). After these two pic & vid are my pics & vids.
Inside the harbor, docks wrecked, boats damaged, some heavily.
Debris floating in and out with the swell.
The shtters in the river! The wharf bathroom is a brick shthouse, it floated a mile away and lodged itself against the cliff at the mouth of the river.
The beaches are covered with all kinds of debris.
item.dc:creator: MrHappySmiles
